what_it_does: This file is a QA and verification backlog for the Counterpart project: it records live smoke-test results, bugs found and fixed during the sweep, remaining todos, and design-quality directives.
observations: Serving was restored and verified live on 2026-06-08 with a passing smoke test: 15 registries load and all workspace components are functions.
Desktop visual confirmation covered Composition (Asset Lab), Streams, and Generate surfaces with no horizontal overflow.

A bug was fixed during the sweep: enum variables like image.mood were resolving from memory to a raw learning sentence instead of a valid option; resolve.js now constrains memory hits to the option set.
Attune token-default rows were fixed to show human labels (Warm, Calm, Standard, Medium) via MS_DECISIONS.activeOption(...).label.
Remaining work includes a mobile sweep at 390/768 for new surfaces, a visual-quality upgrade pass, and stream-runner/pedagogy completion.
The backlog is organized into sections A–E: smoke test, visual sweep, composition catalog growth, visual-quality upgrade, stream runner, and ongoing documentation/TDD practices.
Shape system C2 is marked done but still needs online visual confirmation for clipped hexagon/octagon/diamond rendering and accent recolouring.
It instructs documenting every new registry/surface in CLAUDE.md, folder AGENTS.md, and db/schema.sql with mirrored schema.
It emphasizes tokenized values (MS_* registries, var(--*)) and dynamic context resolution (MS_RESOLVE), with loud failure and no silent fallback.
standouts: Live verification restored and a concrete enum-resolution bug fixed during the same sweep.
The visual-quality directive calls for diversity, dynamism, nesting, and distinct mobile/desktop-native surfaces rather than mere reflow.
Canonical ConceptV shapes (hexagon/octagon/diamond) are now wired into Composition catalog and Streams cards/launcher.
conventions: Uses markdown status markers: ✅ for done/fixed and STILL TODO for pending items.
Names registries with an MS_* prefix and requires tokenized, registry-driven values.
Requires documentation parity across CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and db/schema.sql.
Prefers TDD for pure logic and live verification for UI/UX.
States design principles directly: no raw hex/px/ms, loud failure, no silent fallback.
concerns: The file mixes verified results, fixed bugs, and aspirational backlog in one long document, which may drift.
Several items are marked DONE logically but still need online visual confirmation, creating ambiguity about true completion.
The visual-quality upgrade pass in section C is broad and lacks concrete acceptance criteria.
Mobile verification for Streams and Generate is explicitly not yet done.
No versioning or date is attached to the remaining TODOs.
notes: This is the live QA backlog that tells you the build is serving, what got fixed during verification, and what mobile, visual, and stream-runner quality work is still queued.
questions: What exact success criteria define the visual-quality upgrade pass in section C?
Which specific surfaces count as NEW surfaces beyond Composition, Streams, and Generate?
How is the responsive harness _qa/responsive-harness.html wired into the build or test flow?
What is the intended relationship between MS_TUTORIALS content and the runner's pedagogical step labels?
Is loud failure already instrumented, or is it only a stated principle?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is an online verification + visual-quality backlog, and the file indeed records verification results, fixed defects, and remaining quality work.
intention_signals: It is trying to establish confidence that the current build is functional and to route the remaining polish work toward mobile verification, visual diversity, and stream-runner pedagogy.
novelty: It combines a traditional QA backlog with a design-direction brief and includes a narrative bug-fix account tied to TDD and live confirmation.
contribution: It contributes a dated snapshot of verified functionality, the rationale and confirmation for two fixed defects, and a prioritized list of remaining QA and visual-quality tasks that a fused system would keep as historical project state.
summary_for_embedding: ONLINE_VERIFICATION.md is a Counterpart project QA backlog that records a 2026-06-08 live smoke-test pass, two fixed bugs around enum variable resolution and token-default display, and remaining todos for mobile responsive verification, visual-quality/diversity upgrades, and stream-runner pedagogy.
intention_for_embedding: It was meant to document what has been verified online, what broke and was fixed during the sweep, and what quality work still needs to be done.
